text.title: TTS Basics — Provider Routing Decision Tree

decision-tree.root-question: Does the payload fit in a single call (len(cleaned_text) <= 4000 chars)?

branch.conclusion: Reject. Route to tts-implementation LongTextTTS chunking — do not silently truncate.

branch.question: Does the payload contain SSML markup (`<break>`, `<prosody>`, `<say-as>`, `<phoneme>`)?

branch.conclusion: Force provider in {google, azure}. Never pass SSML to OpenAI TTS or ElevenLabs — both produce broken audio.

branch.question: Is a specific person's voice required (voice_clone_required == true)?

branch.conclusion: Escalate to tts-implementation ElevenLabs clone_voice path. tts-basics does not handle cloning. Cache key still includes provider=elevenlabs to keep replay safe.

branch.question: Is the language in OpenAI TTS supported set (en, es, fr, de, it, pt, pl, ja, zh, ru, uk, ko, hi, ar, tr, vi)?

branch.conclusion: Fall back to ElevenLabs eleven_multilingual_v2 OR Google Neural2 — pick by cost cap. Apply rate-limit delay per rule r6.

branch.question: Is this a final production asset (vs draft/preview)?

branch.conclusion: OpenAI TTS with model=tts-1-hd. Higher quality justifies 2x cost on shipped audio.
OpenAI TTS with model=tts-1. Iterate cheap; promote to tts-1-hd only on final pass.

example.p: Input: "Markets closed up 2% today." (28 chars, no SSML, no clone, en, draft) → char_count <= 4000 → ssml_present false → voice_clone_required false → lang_supported true → final_asset false → conclusion r1-model-tier: OpenAI tts-1, voice=onyx via VOICE_MAP["news"]["openai"].
